title: CallLint Authority Manifest
description: A normalized capability inventory (calllint.authority.v0) for what authority an artifact would exercise if run: the closed set of capabilities (action x resource), each pinned to the evidence byte that granted it, with mutability/reversibility/approval so the deterministic policy (calllint.decision.v0) can decide over it. Object 3 of the six: subject.artifactDigest binds the Artifact Identity; a later Decision/Plan/Receipt binds this manifest's digest. Authority is NOT a verdict - the manifest never sets SAFE/REVIEW/BLOCK. Fully deterministic; digest = sha256 over this object minus its own digest field. See ADR 0035.

artifactDigest.description: Binds the Artifact Identity (object 1). null only when built over an artifact that did not fully resolve.

capabilities.description: Deterministically ordered capability inventory (action x resource). May be empty.

action.description: What the capability DOES. Fixed vocabulary; extending it is ADR-gated.

resource.description: What the capability acts ON. Fixed vocabulary; extending it is ADR-gated.

scope.description: Where the authority applies ("project", a host, a path, a scope string). null when unbounded or unknown.

destination.description: For send/connect: the outbound destination (host/URL). null otherwise.

mutability.description: Whether exercising the capability changes state.

reversibility.description: How recoverable a mutation is. "n/a" for read-only capabilities.

monetaryLimit.description: For spend: the per-call/total ceiling if declared. null otherwise.

approvalRequirement.description: Normalized sign-off the capability warrants. NOT itself the verdict; the policy reads it. "block" = must not run without explicit digest-bound approval (fail-closed).

evidenceSource.description: Provenance of the capability: "server.url", "server.args[2]", "SKILL.md:42". Every capability is traceable to the byte that granted it (evidence is mandatory).

completeness.description: Whether the capability was fully understood or only partially pinned.

pattern.description: Which of the six instruction-authority patterns produced this capability (instruction-derived capabilities only).

trustSource.description: Trust class of the data at the head of this capability (calllint.authority.v0, ADR 0041). Optional and additive: omitted or "unknown" MUST read as not trusted (never auto-SAFE). Derived deterministically from (action, resource, scope, evidenceSource, pattern); anything not establishable from shipped signals is left "unknown" (fail-safe). Feeds Toxic-Flow analysis (ADR 0040).

limits.description: T10 Safety-Budget limits, folded into authority.

spendPerCall.description: Max spend a single call may incur, if declared; null = undeclared.

spendTotal.description: Max total spend across a session, if declared; null = undeclared.

required.description: Normalized approval labels required across all capabilities (sorted, deduped).

unknowns.description: Sources we could not fully normalize, so the policy never over-trusts silence. Non-empty forces completeness = "partial".

completeness.description: Derived: "partial" if any capability is partial or unknowns is non-empty.

digest.description: sha256 over this object minus its own digest field (hashJson from @calllint/fingerprint).
